title: rhinanthus crista-galli var. fallax
intro: Rhinanthus crista-galli var. fallax, also known as the yellow rattle, is a parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of grasses and other plants. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rhinanthus crista-galli var. fallax are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to the affected area.
